items.title: 'Devoted father and gentleman': Tributes flow for shark attack victim

children.content: The wife of 38-year-old Perth man Steven Mattaboni says her family's "hearts are irrevocably broken" after he was killed in a shark attack while spearfishing off the coast of Rottnest Island on Saturday.

articles.title: Friends witness 'horrific' shark attack that claimed life of spearfishing mate

children.content: A 38-year-old man had been spearfishing with friends when he was killed in a shark attack off Rottnest Island on Saturday morning.

image.altText: Police and ambulance parked near a jetty at a bay

items.title: Bid to reopen Albany hydrotherapy pool rejected

children.content: The WA health minister rejects recommissioning Albany's public hydrotherapy pool despite a feasibility study saying there is a justifiable cause to do so.

image.altText: Older women standing in her lush garden using a walking frame

items.title: Six shops ordered to shut under new illegal tobacco laws

children.content: More than 140,000 illegal cigarettes have been seized and six stores ordered to close down, days after Western Australia's new tobacco laws came into force.

image.altText: A shop front with 'store closed' banners stuck on the front windows

items.title: Harley heroics lift Eagles to stunning upset win over Giants after Dockers equal record

children.content: West Coast surges to a third victory of the season, defeating GWS by 17 points at Perth Stadium, after Fremantle posts a club record-equalling ninth straight win, beating Essendon by 43 points at the MCG.

Pat Voss tugs at his jersey in a goal celebration

items.title: Teenage pedestrian dies after being hit in Perth car park

children.content: A 19-year-old driver is being questioned by police after a teenager is struck by a car in a Perth shopping centre car park and dies in hospital.

image.altText: A car being towed away in a car park

items.title: Cakes and connection as cafe project gives women confidence boost

children.content: Beyond the Kitchen, a course run by Multicultural Services Centre of WA, gives women from migrant, refugee and asylum seeker backgrounds a safe space to develop friendships and hospitality business skills.

image.altText: Women cook food in a kitchen

items.title: Reef restoration boosts Swan River's health

children.content: Work is underway to restore Perth's Swan River and help its severely degraded riverbed, where 1 million tonnes of limestone was taken in the 1990s.

gallery.altText: Man in black puffer with bushland in the background
An excavator on a boat shovelling limestone bricks into the Swan River.

An excavator on a boat shovelling limestone bricks into the Swan River.

items.title: Tourism operators priced out of own industry conference

children.content: Struggling businesses in Carnarvon, reliant on tourism, say they want more support to be involved with the 2026 WA Tourism Conference that the region is hosting.

items.title: Why the US, UK and NZ haven't raised interest rates in years

children.content: As Australians pay more on their mortgages, some countries have had no rise in their interest rate in recent years. So what's going on?

image.altText: Three balloons with the UK, NZ and US flags wrapped around them rise against a lined background

items.title: Tax is a great motivator but will Labor's changes fix Australia's housing mess?

children.content: The Albanese government and the opposition both want to turn back the clock on the Australian tax system.

image.altText: Albanese is in focus as he sits on the green bench watching Chalmers speak

items.title: Mass showers and squalor: What spurred ex-Labor minister's NDIS fight

children.content: A former NSW Labor powerbroker and state disability minister pivotal in advocating for the establishment of the NDIS has warned against rushing through sweeping changes to the scheme.

image.altText: A middle aged man with short grey hair sitting on a verandah

items.title: Push for Australia to extract helium as Iran war impacts global supply

children.content: Helium is needed in health care and to make microchips and semiconductors, but the Middle East war has affected supply. Some think it is an opportunity for Australia's gas industry.

items.title: WA's first pair of fainting goats settle into new home

children.content: The goats, which "faint" when stressed or excited, have travelled to a farm near Busselton, where they will be bred as pets.

items.title: The $10b offer Dave Hughes would 'hand on heart' turn down

children.content: Comedian Dave Hughes spoke to The Assembly about why he quit alcohol at 22, being "addicted to work" and the type of dad he's trying to be for his three kids.

items.title: Late bogey costs Cameron Smith after PGA Championship surge

children.content: Australian Cameron Smith makes a hot start to the final round at the PGA Championship, putting himself right in the mix to end his major drought, before tripping up on the penultimate hole.

image.altText: Cameron Smith gestures with his hand after putting at the PGA Championship.

items.title: Quiz: It's history time! Where was Pac-Man first released?

children.content: In what year did John Howard become a federal MP? And which country got to play Pac-Man first? Test yourself in this week's history quiz.

image.altText: Neon lights in the shape of Pac-man (a yellow circle with a missing segment for a mouth) and two ghosts, in pink and blue.

items.title: ASX set to fall after sharp drops on Wall Street, AUD down

children.content: Australian markets are set to begin the week in negative territory, with ASX 200 futures pointing to a drop of around 0.1 per cent on Monday after both US indexes fell sharply from record highs. Follow the day's financial news and insights from our specialist business reporters on our live blog.

image.altText: A woman is seen in the reflection of a window looking at shares' performance on a board of the ASX

items.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship Australians unlikely to be paid out, experts say

children.content: But the lawyer who led the class action in the COVID-19 era Ruby Princess case has warned the cruise company will likely argue Australian law had no jurisdiction over the matter.

articles.title: Passengers from hantavirus ship transferred to Australian quarantine

children.content: A flight carrying six people who were on board the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship lands at an air force base in Perth.

items.title: Kalgoorlie's 'shovel ready' water project misses out in federal budget

children.content: A project designed to drought-proof Australia's biggest outback city has missed out on funding in this week's federal budget, with the City of Kalgoorlie-Boulder set to review its ambitious plans for what has been dubbed Water Bank.

image.altText: A drone photograph of Kalgoorlie-Boulder in regional Australia.

items.title: The dilemma Wallabies and All Blacks face when players head overseas

children.content: Both Australia and New Zealand strive to protect Super Rugby Pacific as their premier domestic competition, even it if it has consequences for the Wallabies and All Blacks.

items.title: Video of shark decapitation sparks calls for better protection

children.content: A researcher speaks out against the fishing of sharks after footage of a huge tiger shark being caught and decapitated circulated on social media.

image.altText: hands holding large shark's mouth open.

items.title: Diphtheria is back in Australia. Here's what to know

children.content: Once a major cause of childhood deaths, diphtheria was largely eliminated through vaccination. Health authorities say falling immunity may now be helping the disease spread again.

image.altText: A young child gets there nose blown.

items.title: How once revered donkeys became a 'nuisance' and meat for cans

children.content: Before roads and vehicles, teams of donkeys used to haul huge loads across remote parts of Western Australia. But the beloved animal became a declared pest that even the meat trade couldn't save.
